cover:
A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "FLOW vs APEX IN 2026: When to Use Which — The Architectural Decision Framework".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "FLOW vs APEX IN 2026" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "When to Use Which — The Architectural Decision Framework".

Left Column (✓ USE FLOW WHEN...):
- Header: "✓ USE FLOW WHEN..." in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Logic is declarative, linear, and easy to visualize" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Admins or business users need to adjust it regularly" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Screen-guided user input or approval routing is needed" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Field updates, notifications, and basic routing apply" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Agentforce 2.0 needs admin-configurable process steps" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Moderate volume — no nested loops or complex joins" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (✓ USE APEX WHEN...):
- Header: "✓ USE APEX WHEN..." in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Logic is algorithmic, high-volume, or multi-object" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "You need deterministic behavior and serious unit tests" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Reusable domain services shared across triggers, LWC, agents" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Advanced transaction control, dynamic SOQL, bulk DML" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Exposing safe, typed, tested actions to Agentforce 2.0" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Flow would become a 300-node maze nobody wants to touch" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.
images:
A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "FLOW vs APEX: Real Production Scenarios: 8 Decision Points — Which Tool Wins and Why".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "FLOW vs APEX: Real Production Scenarios" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "8 Decision Points — Which Tool Wins and Why".

Left Column (FLOW WINS):
- Header: "FLOW WINS" in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Lead routing rules that change every quarter → admins own it" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Screen wizard for guided renewal opportunity creation" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Approval escalation with manager notification + task creation" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Agentforce 2.0 triggered process: collect missing billing data" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Record-triggered: if Country = Germany → apply DACH sales path" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Fault email to ops team when exception path fires" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (APEX WINS):
- Header: "APEX WINS" in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "SLA recalculation across Critical / High / Standard tiers" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Bulk case update: 200+ records, premium tier checks, queue routing" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Dynamic SOQL with multi-object joins and conditional filters" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Invocable action reused by Flow, LWC, Queueable, and agent" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Logic requiring 20+ test methods to trust in production" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Transactional write with idempotency key + audit log insert" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.
